scan_metadata.scan_method: ast.walk + recursive descent (NOT ast.iter_child_nodes which misses nested defs in if/for/with blocks)
scan_metadata.verification: Dual-core cross-checked: bailian (19) + kimi (19) - 100% identical results
scan_metadata.impact_on_d_plus_count: None - all 19 nested functions are CC ≤ 11 (B grade or below)

functions.name: init_project_memory._dry_run_action
functions.note: Inside `if dry_run:` block - missed by iter_child_nodes

nested_functions_detail.note: All 3 missed in previous scan due to iter_child_nodes limitation

radon_limitation_note.limitation: radon cc does NOT report nested functions (functions defined inside another function's body, including those inside if/for/with/try blocks)
radon_limitation_note.impact_on_d_plus: None. All 19 nested functions have CC ≤ 11 (B grade). No D+ candidates hidden in nested scope.
radon_limitation_note.impact_on_function_count: Significant. Total FunctionDef in 5 audit-focus files is 194 (not 175 top-level only). Previous counts of '184 functions' were undercounted by 19.
radon_limitation_note.workaround: Use ast.walk(tree) instead of ast.iter_child_nodes(tree) to catch all nested defs at any depth.

previous_scan_error_log.impact: Missed 8 nested functions (those inside if/for/with blocks, not direct children of FunctionDef)
previous_scan_error_log.fix: Switched to ast.walk which recurses through all child nodes

previous_scan_error_log.what: Trusted radon function count as authoritative
previous_scan_error_log.fix: AST is ground truth for function definitions; radon is ground truth for CC values of top-level functions only
